task_completion.description: Vital Recorder application remains open and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder 1.16.6 application window is visible and in focus on the desktop.

task_completion.description: The correct file '0001.vital' remains loaded in the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename '0001.vital' is visible in the application's title bar or main interface.

task_completion.description: The agent accesses the UI control to change the display mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A menu (such as 'View' or 'Mode') or a toolbar dropdown is opened by the agent to access display options.

task_completion.description: The agent selects the Monitor mode option.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cursor is hovering over or clicking an option labeled 'Monitor' or 'Monitor Mode' in the menu or toolbar.

task_completion.description: The application successfully switches to Monitor mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main display layout changes from the horizontal Track mode to Monitor mode, which typically resembles a clinical patient monitor with large numerical parameter values displayed alongside the real-time scrolling waveforms.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to switch modes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Mouse cursor movements and clicks on the Vital Recorder menus/buttons are visible, with no terminal commands or scripts used to force the state change.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Vital Recorder software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches Vital Recorder 1.16.6, and the waveforms displayed correspond to the clinical parameters described (ART, ECG, PLETH, CO2, AWP) rather than a static image or mockup.

integrity.description: The agent did not open a different file or alter the underlying data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The loaded file remains '0001.vital' throughout the trajectory, and no other files are opened or substituted.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task operates on '0001.vital', a real data file from the VitalDB open dataset (exactly 21,063,820 bytes). It contains actual waveform traces from a noncardiac surgery at Seoul National University Hospital. The VLM should verify the presence of these specific clinical parameters in the interface: arterial pressure (ART, red), ECG leads (ECG_II, ECG_V5, green), pulse oximetry (PLETH, light blue), capnography (CO2, yellow), and airway pressure (AWP, white).
